title: Reading latitude and longitude coordinates
problem: Buenos Aires, Argentina, is located at approximately $34°\text{S},\ 58°\text{W}$. (a) In which hemisphere (north or south) is Buenos Aires? (b) Is Buenos Aires east or west of the Prime Meridian? (c) State the latitude as a positive number of degrees.

steps.instruction: Read the latitude coordinate and identify the hemisphere.
steps.working: The latitude is $34°\text{S}$. The letter S indicates south of the equator, so Buenos Aires is in the Southern hemisphere.

steps.instruction: Read the longitude coordinate and identify the direction from the Prime Meridian.
steps.working: The longitude is $58°\text{W}$. The letter W indicates west of the Prime Meridian ($0°$), so Buenos Aires is west of the Prime Meridian.

steps.instruction: State the latitude as a positive number.
steps.working: The latitude is $34°\text{S}$, so the positive value is $34°$.

answer: (a) Southern hemisphere; (b) West of the Prime Meridian; (c) $34°$

title: Calculating time difference from longitudes
problem: City P is at longitude $30°\text{E}$ and City Q is at longitude $105°\text{E}$. Using the rule that $15°$ of longitude corresponds to $1$ hour of time difference, find the time difference between the two cities. If it is $06{:}00$ in City P, what is the local time in City Q?

steps.instruction: Find the difference in longitude between the two cities.
Divide the longitude difference by 15 to find the time difference in hours.
Determine which city is ahead. The city further east has the later local time.
steps.working: City Q ($105°\text{E}$) is east of City P ($30°\text{E}$), so City Q is $5$ hours ahead.

steps.instruction: Add the time difference to the time in City P to find the local time in City Q.

answer: The time difference is $5$ hours. It is $11{:}00$ in City Q.

title: Local arrival time for a westward flight
problem: A flight departs from City A at longitude $75°\text{E}$ at $10{:}00$ local time and flies west to City B at longitude $15°\text{W}$. The flight takes $9$ hours. Using $15°$ of longitude $= 1$ hour, determine the local time in City B when the plane arrives.

steps.instruction: Find the longitude difference. Since City A is east and City B is west, add the two longitude values.
Calculate the time difference and note the direction.
steps.working:
$$\text{Time difference} = 90° \div 15 = 6 \text{ hours}$$
City B is west of City A, so City B is $6$ hours behind City A.

steps.instruction: Find the arrival time in City A's local time by adding the flight duration to the departure time.
Convert to City B's local time by subtracting the time difference (City B is behind).

answer: The plane arrives at $13{:}00$ local time in City B.
